title: pavonia nigrobracteata
intro: Pavonia nigrobracteata, also known as the Black-bracted Pavonia or Black-bracted Rosemallow, is a plant species in the family Malvaceae. It is a shrub-like plant that grows up to 3 feet tall, with large, showy flowers and dark leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, salves, and tinctures, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and soothing skin irritations.
